stories.title: **Headline:** Ramirez Wins AL Player of the Month for April — .342 BA, 7 HR, 28 RBI in 21 Games

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
José Ramírez won AL Player of the Month for April.

.342 BA. .722 SLG. 7 HR. 28 RBI. 9 walks. 21 games.

He set the Cleveland franchise record for games played (1,620) on April 6.

The most underrated superstar in baseball.

#Guardians #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
José Ramírez is tracking for his THIRD consecutive 30-30 season.

That would make him the ninth player in major league history to record at least two consecutive 30-30 seasons.

He is 35 years old and somehow getting better.

#Guardians #MLB

article.headline: José Ramírez Wins AL Player of the Month: .342, 7 HR, 28 RBI — and a Third 30-30 Season in Sight

stories.title: **Headline:** Red Sox Go 2-0 Under Chad Tracy — Is the Turnaround Real?

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Chad Tracy's first two games as Red Sox interim manager:

— 5-3 win over Baltimore
— 5-0 shutout of defending AL champion Toronto

Boston was 10-17 under Alex Cora.

Early returns are promising. But there's a lot of ground to make up.

#RedSox #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
The Red Sox fired Alex Cora. Named a Triple-A manager to replace him.

And somehow — so far — it's working.

Chad Tracy is 2-0. That's not a turnaround yet.

But it's something.

#RedSox #MLB

article.headline: Red Sox Go 2-0 Under Chad Tracy — Can a Managerial Change Actually Save Boston's Season?

stories.title: **Headline:** Murakami's Unique Plate Profile — MLB HR Leader Walks, Strikes Out, or Homers — Nothing Else

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Munetaka Murakami walks, strikes out, or hits a monster homer.

That's the whole plate appearance.

His first 12 MLB extra-base hits were ALL home runs — record since 1900.

He leads MLB in HRs. The White Sox have found something special.

#WhiteSox #MLB

stories.title: **Headline:** NL Central All Five Teams Still Above .500 Entering May — Historic Race

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Every single NL Central team is above .500 entering May.

This is the second-latest date in baseball history that's happened in any division.

Three playoff spots. Five contenders.

Someone's going to miss the postseason with a winning record. 😬

#MLB #NLCentral

stories.title: **Headline:** Judge's 400-HR Chase — On Pace to Make History in 2026

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Aaron Judge entered 2026 needing ~32 HRs to join the 400-home run club.

He has 12 through April.

5 of his first 9 HRs came in the FIRST INNING — with a .450 BA and 1.726 OPS in that slot.

He could reach 400 before September.

#Yankees #MLB #AaronJudge

stories.title: **Headline:** Ohtani's On-Base Streak at 48 Games — Two-Way April Ends with Elite Numbers

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Shohei Ohtani's on-base streak is at 48 consecutive games — the third-longest in Dodgers franchise history.

April wrap: sub-0.40 ERA pitching. .996 OPS hitting. 5 HR. Dodgers 20-9, tied for ML-best.

No historical comparison exists for what he's doing.

#Dodgers #Ohtani #MLB

stories.title: **Headline:** May Watch List — Five Names to Track as Summer Heats Up

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text:
Five MLB storylines to watch in May:

🔹 Murakami's HR pace (60+?)
🔹 Judge's 400-HR chase
🔹 Ramírez's 3rd consecutive 30-30
🔹 NL Central's 5-team race (3 playoff spots)
🔹 Red Sox under Tracy — turnaround or collapse?

May is going to be 🔥

#MLB
